access.anon: On the home page, can only see the public bus and frequent links views; cannot enter the personal overview.
access.user: The home page serves as the personal workspace.
access.admin: The home page personal workspace behavior is the same as regular users.
access.agent: Can read overview results with the same semantics as the home page; REST and MCP should return consistent overview information.

rules.decision-page: The home page is not a directory page but a decision page for 'what to do next'.
rules.now-next-first: The authenticated overview begins with the current or next actionable schedule, homework, exam, or todo in a rolling seven-day window. Today's urgent work follows it, while frequent links remain secondary and appear after planning content.
rules.current-semester-priority: The above-the-fold area prioritizes personal learning tasks within the current semester, not historical semester content or complete directory information.
rules.homework-todo-layered: Homework and todos are layered as 'due today / due soon / all incomplete'; homework with no due date does not participate in upcoming due date sorting.
rules.upcoming-exam-counts: Upcoming exam counts are shared between the dashboard web navigation, REST overview, and MCP overview. They include known-date exams that have not ended or start later today, and exclude date-unknown exam records that cannot be placed in the upcoming timeline.
rules.non-current-not-disguised: Non-current-semester subscribed sections must not be disguised as current learning tasks.
rules.aggregate-first-for-assistants: Prefer a single aggregated snapshot tool for common assistant prompts before fanning out into multiple narrower tool calls.
rules.historical-subscriptions-remain-discover: A zero current-semester subscription count must not be presented as no course history when total subscriptions remain; web and assistant surfaces must offer a path to past-term sections, homework, schedules, and exams.
rules.focused-extracts-share-window: Focused tools such as next-class and upcoming-deadlines should stay aligned with the same short-horizon window as the broader dashboard snapshot.
rules.dashboard-card-priority: Dashboard cards prioritize the actionable object title, timing/status, and direct destination over raw nested identifiers. Secondary context such as course, section, campus, or priority badge remains visible for disambiguation; platform audit timestamps and repeated parent objects stay out of compact cards and MCP default output.

routes.returns: { user, anchor, counts, schedules, todos, dueTodos, homeworks, exams }
notes: Compact authenticated overview for bot, CLI, and mobile clients.
Supports atTime, homeworkWindowDays, limit, and locale query parameters; date-only atTime values anchor to the start of that Asia/Shanghai day.
Includes today schedule samples and incomplete todo samples in addition to the due-todo, homework, and exam samples exposed by get_my_overview.

tools.name: get_my_overview
notes: overview contains pending todo, homework, today's schedule, and upcoming exam counts.
Accepts atTime, homeworkWindowDays, limit, and locale with the same bounds and sample-window semantics as GET /api/me/overview; date-only atTime values anchor to the start of that Asia/Shanghai day.
Default/full samples contain dueTodos[], dueHomeworks[], and upcomingExams[] from the same compact overview read operation.

The deprecated summary input is an alias for the same compact dueTodos[], dueHomeworks[], and upcomingExams[] structure returned by default; use get_my_dashboard for a richer single-call assistant snapshot.

tools.name: get_my_dashboard
tools.returns: { user, currentSemester, subscriptions, nextClass, upcomingDeadlines, upcomingEvents, todos, bus }
notes: Preferred single-call snapshot for assistant workflows.

Overlaps get_my_overview but adds currentSemester, currentSemesterSections, nextClass, merged deadlines, and preferred-bus departures; accepts optional atTime to anchor those time windows instead of using the server clock. When subscriptions.totalCount is greater than subscriptions.currentSemesterCount, past-term follows remain available through list_my_subscribed_sections and the semesterId filters on list_my_homeworks, list_my_schedules, and list_my_exams.
Default mode should stay assistant-compact; request full mode when the caller explicitly needs full nested schedule, room, teacher, or homework payloads.
The deprecated summary input is an alias for default and must preserve the same top-level dashboard collections rather than introducing alternate wrapper shapes.

tools.name: get_next_class
notes: Focused extract for 'what is my next class?' prompts.
Accepts optional atTime to anchor the lookup instead of using the server clock.

tools.name: get_upcoming_deadlines
notes: Merges homework_due, exam, and todo_due entries into one ordered list.
Accepts optional atTime to anchor the deadline window instead of using the server clock.
